text.title: Cache Write Patterns (Cache-Aside, Write-Through, Write-Behind) Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Cache Write Patterns (Cache-Aside, Write-Through, Write-Behind) application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Cache-aside (lazy load) is the default pattern; deviations require explicit justification.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Caching pattern catalogues. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Write-through MUST be atomic: DB and cache succeed together OR neither writes (transactional outbox).
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Distributed-systems best practice. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Write-behind buffer MUST have a bounded queue + back-pressure to the writer on overflow.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Queueing-theory reviews. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Cache writes happen AFTER the DB write succeeds, not before — never populate cache speculatively.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Field manuals. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: On write, DELETE the cache key instead of UPDATING it — lazy re-population avoids stale composite bugs.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Memcached + Redis best-practice docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Cache write failures MUST retry up to N times then alert — never silently drop.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: SRE workbook. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Write-behind to a message bus MUST use the transactional outbox pattern to avoid lost writes.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Microservices patterns book. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
